steps.title: Use the discriminant test
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solutions when its discriminant is negative. For
$$-x^2+bx-49=0$$
the coefficients are $a=-1$, $b=b$, and $c=-49$.

steps.title: Check the boundary integers
steps.content: Since $196=14^2$, the condition is $|b|\lt14$, or $-14\lt b\lt14$. The least integer in that interval is $-13$.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: The least possible integer value of $b$ is -13.
